title: Nutanix Prism Central Alert Monitoring
exclusions: Single-cluster Prism Element deployments without Prism Central should poll the Prism Element v2 `GET/POST` family such as /api/nutanix/v2.0/alerts (not the PC v3 /api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list contract) in a different UC. Nutanix Files, Objects, Frame, and Era operational alerts use other API families (/api/files/v3, /api/objects/v3, /api/era/v0 patterns, and Frame-specific v3) and belong to separate UCs. Nutanix Calm application blueprints, Move (Xtract) migration appliances, Foundation/Phoenix imaging sessions, and Karbon Kubernetes control-plane alerts are different APIs or different Splunk TAs. Hardware sensors that originate only in Cisco UCS CIMC or iDRAC inband agents rather than from Prism’s alert service stay in the Cisco or hardware UC set for those platforms. This UC-19.1.26 is alert-plane only; it explicitly does not implement /api/nutanix/v3/clusters/list (UC-19.1.27 AOS compliance) or /api/nutanix/v3/protection_rules/list (UC-19.1.28 snapshot or RPO compliance).
dataSources: `index=nutanix` (or your org index) and sourcetype in (`nutanix:prism_central:alerts` primary) with defensive ORs to `nutanix:alerts:v3` and `nutanix:alerts` for older Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism (5076) builds. Ingested fields after TA or JSON parsing include: `is_resolved` or `resolved`, `severity` or `alert_severity`, `is_acknowledged`, `impact_types` (multivalue or delimited), `metadata.uuid` (alert id), `cluster_name` or `cluster`, `affected_entity_kind`, `title`, and `creation_time` or event time. Collection uses Prism Central POST `https://<pcvm>:9440/api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list` with body filter `resolved==false`, optional pagination via `length` and `offset`, and HTTP Basic, bearer tokens, or OAuth2-style client credentials as supported by the TA version and your Prism auth model. JSON paths in the v3 spec include entities[].metadata.uuid, entities[].status.severity, entities[].status.impact_types[], entities[].status.resolution_status.is_resolved / is_acknowledged, entities[].status.context_attributes.cluster_name, entities[].status.context_attributes.affected_entity_kind, entities[].status.creation_time, entities[].status.title — the TA may flatten these into single-segment field names. This UC stays on the alarm/alert plane; do not substitute cluster inventory or protection-rule calls.
app: **Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism** (Splunkbase 5076) preferred; OR custom REST API modular input against `/api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list` if the TA is not deployable in the customer environment
spl:
index=nutanix (sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:alerts" OR sourcetype="nutanix:alerts:v3" OR sourcetype="nutanix:alerts")
| eval v=coalesce(is_resolved, resolved, "false")
| where v==false OR lower(tostring(v)) IN ("false","0","f","no")
| eval sev=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(severity, alert_severity, ""))))
| eval ack=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(is_acknowledged, acknowledged, "false"))))
| eval is_ack=if(ack IN ("true","1","t","yes"), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(match(sev,"^(critical|c)$"),"critical", match(sev,"^(warning|warn|w)$") AND is_ack=0,"warning", match(sev,"^(warning|warn|w)$") AND is_ack=1,"watch", match(sev,"^(info|i)$"),"info", 1=1,"info")
| eval it_coerced=if(mvcount(impact_types)>=1, impact_types, split(tostring(coalesce(impact_type, impact_types, "")), "[,;|]"))
| eval top_impact=case(isnotnull(mvfind(it_coerced, "(?i)Availability")),"Availability", isnotnull(mvfind(it_coerced, "(?i)Capacity")),"Capacity", isnotnull(mvfind(it_coerced, "(?i)Configuration")),"Configuration", isnotnull(mvfind(it_coerced, "(?i)Performance")),"Performance", 1=1,coalesce(mvindex(it_coerced,0),"unknown"))
| eval cluster=if(len(tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, "")))>0, tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, "")), "__pcvm_self__")
| eval alert_id=coalesce('metadata.uuid', metadata_uuid, alert_uuid, id, "")
| eval alert_id=if(alert_id=="" OR isnull(alert_id), tostring(cluster) + "_" + md5(_raw), alert_id)
| sort 0 - _time
| dedup alert_id
| stats count as alert_count, max(_time) as latest_event_time, values(title) as sample_titles by cluster, severity_bucket, top_impact
| eval sample_titles=mvindex(sample_titles,0,2)
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical",4, severity_bucket="warning",3, severity_bucket="watch",2, 1)
| sort 0 - sev_order - alert_count
| table cluster, severity_bucket, top_impact, latest_event_time, alert_count, sample_titles
description: Prism Central aggregates /api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list (POST, JSON `filter` of `resolved==false`, v3 `kind: alert`, paginated with `length` and `offset` against `metadata.total_matches`) so one PC control plane can surface active AOS- and CVM-originated alarms across all registered clusters. A Splunk modular input (community Splunk Add-on for Nutanix on 5076) or a scripted poller should normalize nested entities[] v3 fields—metadata.uuid as stable id, status.severity (often upper case from the API) case-folded in SPL, status.impact_types as a multivalue list, and status.resolution_status booleans for resolved vs acknowledged. The search keeps sourcetype compatibility across TA generations with an OR of `nutanix:prism_central:alerts`, `nutanix:alerts:v3`, and `nutanix:alerts`, then rebuilds a four-level severity_bucket that treats “acknowledged but still unresolved” as watch rather than a clean bill of health, and it rolls up to a per-cluster, per-bucket, per-top-impact line using mvfind-ordered impact_types so dashboards show Availability before Capacity, and so on. Sibling UCs: UC-19.1.27 covers fleet /api/nutanix/v3/clusters/list (AOS compliance); UC-19.1.28 covers /api/nutanix/v3/protection_rules/list (retention or RPO posture) — this JSON does not implement those API families.
value: One Prism Central instance can see every AOS cluster in the estate; operations otherwise jumps between N Prism Element consoles or N per-cluster inboxes, which delays recognition of a cross-fleet incident such as a shared top-of-rack or storage fabric fault, or a synchronized certificate expiry, or a run of CRITICALs during a change window that was supposed to be quiet. A single Splunk view that de-duplicates alert UUIDs, preserves acknowledged-but-unresolved state, and groups by `cluster` plus impact tells incident command whether they have one noisy tenant cluster or a correlated multi-cluster event—shrinking MTTR and avoiding duplicate bridges. Pair the alert title with your CMDB or asset owner context (outside this JSON) to route hardware vs hypervisor vs storage-on-call. This dashboard is the fleet alarm pane; it is not a substitute for the upcoming UC-19.1.27 AOS drift or UC-19.1.28 protection-rule posture searches.
implementation: Install Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism (5076) on a collection tier, create the Prism Central alerts modular input to POST /api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list with `resolved==false`, index to `nutanix` with a sourcetype the TA documents (defensively OR in SPL). Create a read-only Prism user with Cluster Viewer role, store credentials in the Splunk password store, prefer CA-trusted TLS to 9440, then save the use-case SPL as a scheduled report and a five-minute alert with throttling on cluster and bucket.
visualization: Single value: open critical bucket count. Timechart: new alert_id rate by hour and top_impact. Status grid: cluster × severity_bucket. Table: `cluster`, `severity_bucket`, `top_impact`, `latest_event_time`, `alert_count`, `sample_titles` with drilldown to `alert_id`. Optional map or rack overlay only if you join CMDB in a child dashboard, not in base SPL.

references.title: Nutanix Prism Central API Reference (v3 — alerts & general)

knownFalsePositives: Planned LCM (Life Cycle Manager) AOS or firmware rollouts that temporarily elevate alert counts while nodes drain and reboot. Expected rolling CVM restarts during AOS maintenance releases. Deliberate operator disk_failure simulations / RMA dry runs that open synthetic CRITICALs. nfs_storage_io_warnings or snapshot-consolidation storms during large protection-group jobs. First-run backup integration (HYCU, Veeam, Rubrik, or other vendors) that surfaces WARNINGs until schedules stabilize. network_path_redundancy_lost or similar network warnings during a documented, approved ToR or switch MOP. pcsd or cluster-service messages during a documented PCVM scale-out add that includes rolling restarts. User-initiated cluster rename in a lab that re-triggers re-registration noise that typically clears in minutes. vdisk capacity-class WARNINGs/CRITICALs during planned vdisk moves or vStorage migrations. cvm restart-family alerts when an operator intentionally re-tags the CVM management VLAN or bounces a CVM in break-fix with change control. Transient false spikes during IPv6 or name-resolution experiments on the PCVM if the site toggles internal DNS. None of these replace genuine CRITICALs—validate against change calendars before muting long term.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Deploy one working **Prism Central** PCVM (virtual appliance) reachable on TCP 9440 from the Splunk heavy forwarder or search tier that will run the modular input, or from a mid-tier collection host that forwards with the HTTP Event Collector.
• Install the **Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism** (Splunkbase 5076) on a Heavy Forwarder or on a cloud connecter that supports modular inputs; alternatively follow the custom curl scripted-input path below if security policy blocks that TA.
• Create a least-privilege Prism Central local user (for example splunk_alerts_reader) with read-only visibility to alerts across registered clusters; do not reuse a break-glass admin.
• Decide an index name (this document uses index=nutanix, which matches many TA defaults). If you send to main, change every SPL index= stanza consistently.
• Keep this use case on the alarm plane only: `/api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list` POST with JSON filter resolved==false. Fleet-wide AOS version compliance is UC-19.1.27 against /api/nutanix/v3/clusters/list; snapshot retention / RPO posture is UC-19.1.28 against /api/nutanix/v3/protection_rules/list.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

Service account (Prism Central UI): open **Settings → Local User Management** and add a local user splunk_alerts_reader with role **Cluster Viewer** (read-only). Store the password in Splunk’s credential store, not in plain inputs.conf. If your org uses directory services, map an AD group with equivalent read-only Prism RBAC instead of a long-lived local password.

Transport and TLS: Prism Central presents HTTPS on port 9440 with a Nutanix-signed or enterprise-issued certificate. Default lab builds often ship a self-signed chain. Preferred production practice: import the PCVM issuing CA into the OS trust store on the collection host and keep SSL verification enabled. If you must disable verification temporarily during a pilot, scope that to a lab and plan a fix before production; never treat verify_ssl=false as a permanent control.

Option A — Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism (5076): after installation, the add-on’s README lists the exact modular input stanza name (names drift between 2.x and 3.x builds). In $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<TA_name>/local/inputs.conf, configure the Prism Central alerts input (wording in the UI may be "Prism Central Alerts" or "PC Alerts v3") with: target FQDN or IP of the PCVM, port 9440, HTTPS, polling interval 300 seconds (5 minutes) as a balanced default, credentials via **Settings → Data inputs** → the add-on’s entry → credential dropdown, and index=nutanix. The TA should POST /api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list with a filter such as "resolved==false" to reduce noise. If the UI offers page size, set length=250 and enable pagination until metadata total_matches is satisfied, or confirm the TA’s internal loop does the same; otherwise you silently cap at the first page.

```ini
# Example pattern — replace <TA_DIR> with the add-on’s folder on your SH/HF, and
# match stanza names to the version you installed (see the TA’s default/inputs.conf).
[<vendor_specific_stanza_for_prism_central_alerts_v3>]
disabled = 0
prism_central_host = pcvm01.example.corp
prism_central_port = 9440
use_ssl = 1
verify_ssl = 1
ca_file = /opt/splunk/etc/apps/TA_nutanix_local/certs/prism_central_chain.pem
interval = 300
index = nutanix
# credential = (managed in Splunk Manager; do NOT paste secrets here)
```

Option B — Custom scripted input on Linux that shells out to curl every 300s and prints one JSON event per alert line to stdout for Splunk to index (Heavy Forwarder recommended):

$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-PC-alerts/local/inputs.conf
```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-PC-alerts/bin/pc_alerts_pull.sh]
interval = 300
sourcetype = nutanix:prism_central:alerts
index = nutanix
disabled = 0
```

bin/pc_alerts_pull.sh (sketch; add robust pagination, curl exit checks, and secret retrieval from a protected file or vault client):
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
PC="https://pcvm01.example.corp:9440/api/nutanix/v3/alerts/list"
# Body asks only for unresolved rows; add paging with offset in a loop in production.
curl -sS -k -u "${PC_USER:?}:${PC_PASS:?}" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -X POST "$PC" -d '{"filter":"resolved==false","kind":"alert","length":250,"offset":0}'
```

props.conf hints on the index tier if you must parse raw curl output (the TA may already do this for you):

```ini
[nutanix:prism_central:alerts]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = json
# If the event is the full v3 /list response, use spath or a custom extraction.
```

Sample raw v3 entities[] record shape you should see inside the list response (field names the TA will flatten; paths help you build extractions if you roll your own parser):

```json
{
  "metadata": { "uuid": "af7e2c0d-0d3d-4c6b-9d7e-111111111111" },
  "status": {
    "severity": "CRITICAL",
    "impact_types": ["Availability", "Performance"],
    "resolution_status": { "is_resolved": false, "is_acknowledged": false },
    "context_attributes": { "cluster_name": "NUTANIX-PROD-A", "affected_entity_kind": "cvm" },
    "creation_time": "2026-04-26T10:15:00Z",
    "title": "CVM service health degraded"
  }
}
```

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

Run the saved SPL below. It deliberately ORs three sourcetype labels seen across TA generations so a customer on Splunk Add-on for Nutanix 2.x or 3.x still matches without editing the first line. The first stages enforce is_resolved false with both boolean and string shapes because JSON extraction sometimes lands as a string. Severity and ack normalization uses lower() on coalesce() output so CRITICAL, Critical, and critical collapse consistently. The four severity_bucket values separate acknowledged-but-still-broken work (watch) from fresh warnings: an operator may acknowledge a warning during bridge triage; that is not a healthy steady state.

impact_types can arrive as a multivalue field from the TA or as a single comma-delimited string from a lighter parser; it_coerced forces a multivalue list, then mvfind picks the first high-signal business impact in Availability → Capacity → Configuration → Performance order, falling back to the first list element. metadata.uuid (quoted as 'metadata.uuid' in SPL because of the dot) is the stable alert identifier across polls; dedup keeps the newest poll for each id so a long-lived unresolved alert does not duplicate rows. The final stats layer rolls up to one row per cluster, severity bucket, and chosen top impact, carrying latest_event_time and a short sample_titles multivalue for dashboard context.

```spl
index=nutanix (sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:alerts" OR sourcetype="nutanix:alerts:v3" OR sourcetype="nutanix:alerts")
| eval v=coalesce(is_resolved, resolved, "false")
| where v==false OR lower(tostring(v)) IN ("false","0","f","no")
| eval sev=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(severity, alert_severity, ""))))
| eval ack=lower(trim(tostring(coalesce(is_acknowledged, acknowledged, "false"))))
| eval is_ack=if(ack IN ("true","1","t","yes"), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(match(sev,"^(critical|c)$"),"critical", (match(sev,"^(warning|warn|w)$")) AND is_ack=0,"warning", (match(sev,"^(warning|warn|w)$")) AND is_ack=1,"watch", match(sev,"^(info|i)$"),"info", 1=1,"info")
| eval it_coerced=if(mvcount(impact_types)>=1, impact_types, split(tostring(coalesce(impact_type, impact_types, "")), "[,;|]"))
| eval top_impact=case(isnotnull(mvfind(it_coerced, "(?i)Availability")),"Availability", isnotnull(mvfind(it_coerced, "(?i)Capacity")),"Capacity", isnotnull(mvfind(it_coerced, "(?i)Configuration")),"Configuration", isnotnull(mvfind(it_coerced, "(?i)Performance")),"Performance", 1=1,coalesce(mvindex(it_coerced,0),"unknown"))
| eval cluster=if(len(tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, "")))>0, tostring(coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, "")), "__pcvm_self__")
| eval alert_id=coalesce('metadata.uuid', metadata_uuid, alert_uuid, id, "")
| eval alert_id=if(alert_id=="" OR isnull(alert_id), cluster."_".md5(_raw), alert_id)
| sort 0 - _time
| dedup alert_id
| stats count as alert_count, max(_time) as latest_event_time, values(title) as sample_titles by cluster, severity_bucket, top_impact
| eval sample_titles=mvindex(sample_titles,0,2)
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical",4, severity_bucket="warning",3, severity_bucket="watch",2, 1)
| sort 0 - sev_order - alert_count
| table cluster, severity_bucket, top_impact, latest_event_time, alert_count, sample_titles
```

Schedule this as a report for dashboards. For streaming alert behavior, keep a version that operates at raw event level before the stats if you need one alert per alert id; the rolled-up form is for fleet posture panels.

**Step 3 — Validate**

(a) In the Prism Central UI Alerts view, open one live CRITICAL or WARNING and copy its metadata UUID. In Splunk, search index=nutanix (sourcetype OR list from Step 2) and filter with `| search "<uuid substring>"` against _raw; you should see the same title text.

(b) Run `| metadata type=sourcetypes index=nutanix` or a five-minute `| tstats count where index=nutanix by sourcetype` to print which exact sourcetype string your deployment emits; if only nutanix:alerts:v3 appears, the OR clause still matches—document the observed string in your runbook for future authors.

(c) Pull five random rows with `| head 5 | table *` and confirm `severity` or `alert_severity`, `cluster` or `cluster_name`, `impact_types`, `affected_entity_kind` or equivalent, `is_resolved`/`resolved`, and `title` (or a nested title field) are present. If a field is missing, open Verbose mode to see whether it is null in _raw; adjust props or add spath in a temporary test search.

(d) In a non-production cluster, follow Nutanix safe-test guidance (for example brief host maintenance that raises a controlled warning) and verify the new alert shows within one poll interval of your modular input.

(e) If the TA also ingests nutanix:prism_central:clusters (sibling feed), run a small lookup-style join on cluster name in a separate investigative search to attach AOS version and cluster UUID for change correlation—do not over-complicate the main UC SPL.

(f) If your scripted input only requests length=250, create more than 250 simultaneous unresolved test alerts in a lab (rare) or lower length temporarily to exercise pagination; confirm offset loops retrieve rows beyond 250. Production environments with fewer than 250 open alerts are common; still verify metadata total_matches handling in the collector code.

(g) If you use API filter resolved==false at collection, compare counts against a one-off POST without that filter; the delta should be historical resolved noise, not a silent failure.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**

Dashboard: top row single value for critical-bucket count in the last hour; second row timechart of new alert_id per hour by top_impact; third row color grid of cluster vs severity_bucket; primary table of cluster, severity_bucket, top_impact, latest_event_time, alert_count, and sample_titles with token drilldown to a detail search on alert_id and cluster.

Alert: cron `*/5 * * * *` (every five minutes), schedule window last 2 hours, trigger when sev_order is 3 or 4 after recomputing at row level in an alert-specific clone of the SPL (or when critical count > 0 in a summary search). Throttle on cluster and severity_bucket to avoid one storm paging hundreds of times. Set severity to major in ITSM integrations. Webhook or Scripted Alert payload should include alert_id, cluster, title, and a deep link to the Prism Central alert if your portal supports UUID deep links. Record owner (platform team), Sev-1 bridge policy, and link to the Nutanix RMA process for hardware classes.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

(1) Search returns no rows but the UI shows open alerts. Check index name (TA default nutanix vs your main), sourcetype OR list, and time range. Confirm the modular input is running on a Heavy Forwarder that can reach 9440 and that the credential is not locked.

(2) Severity flips case between events. The SPL already lower()-cases coalesce(severity, alert_severity); if your feed uses a third name, add it to the coalesce list.

(3) Duplicate rows every poll. You likely skipped dedup on metadata.uuid; confirm alert_id is non-null. If a subset of events lack uuid, your fallback md5 of cluster+_raw may still collide—prefer fixing extraction.

(4) Connection reset or HTTP 429 from PCVM. Increase interval to 600s, reduce parallel scripts, and verify Prism Central is not under CPU pressure; consider Nutanix Webhook or forwarding integrations if your platform team supports push.

(5) impact_types empty for older AOS. Expect gaps; the SPL falls back to first unknown top_impact and you can add title keyword routing in a forked search for legacy estates.

(6) cluster shows __pcvm_self__. Some platform alerts originate on the PCVM itself, not a registered AOS cluster; relabel in presentation or coalesce with a PCVM FQDN field if your TA provides one.

(7) Karbon, Files, Objects, or Calm alerts intermix. Exclusion is by separate APIs and TAs; if raw feeds overlap, add explicit filters on product-specific identifiers once confirmed in _raw, or keep those workloads out of the nutanix index used for this UC.

(8) Certificate verify failures after PCVM refresh. Re-import the new issuing CA; avoid leaving verify_ssl disabled in production.

(9) Auth failures on day 31. Prism Central local account password policy may expire service users; set non-expiring passwords for automation principals or rotate through your vault quarterly.

(10) Pagination silently truncates. Watch metadata total in raw JSON from the curl test; if total exceeds pulled rows, implement offset loops in the script or use the TA’s documented pagination.

(11) boolean is_resolved ingested as the string "False" with capital F from a legacy parser. The SPL’s v==false** may miss; extend the where clause with `OR lower(tostring(v)) IN ("false")` (already present in the main SPL) — if you see variant strings, add them.
grandmaExplanation: Nutanix Prism Central is one control panel for many small server-and-storage clusters. We watch the warning and serious alerts from all those clusters in one list so a team can see a problem growing before the websites and apps your company runs start getting slow or failing for customers.
hardware: Nutanix Prism Central VM (PCVM) — single-PC 4-vCPU/16GB or scale-out 3-PCVM 6-vCPU/26GB; manages Prism Element clusters running AOS on Nutanix NX/Dell XC/Lenovo HX/HPE DX/Cisco UCS hardware
